title: LED-based characterization of solar cells for underwater applications
author: Zhang, Sartor, et al.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: This protocol describes how the power conversion efficiency (О·) of an underwater solar cell can be characterized in a typical laboratory setting by simulating the underwater spectrum with an LED solar simulator. This type of solar simulator works particularly well for this purpose as it allows for tuning of the irradiance spectrum by varying the intensities of each individual LED (centered at different wavelengths). Using this feature, LEDs can be used to simulate underwater spectra and the performance of a solar cell can be measured under such conditions.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: While we employed an Oriel VeraSol-2 by Newport (FigureВ 1A), any LED solar simulator with LEDs in the spectral range of interest can, in principle, be employed for this purpose. The VeraSol-2 arrives with software allowing for easy adjustment of the output irradiance of each LED in the array (FigureВ 1B), which is important for simulating underwater irradiance spectra (FigureВ 1C). Additionally, up to nine custom irradiance spectra can be saved within the accompanying software, which reduces human error and allows for easy access between the underwater solar spectra. Besides the solar simulator, a source-measure unit (here a Keithley 2400 SMU), a laboratory PC, and a stage (here a lab jack) with an adjustable height are needed.FigureВ 1Before you begin(A) Photograph of the employed experimental setup consisting of the VeraSol-2 setup class AAA solar simulator coupled to a Keithley 2400 SMU.(B) Spectrum control software, allowing for fine tuning each individual LED in the setup.(C) Water-attenuation spectra of various saltwater (S), freshwater (F)В and pure water (P)В sources.(D) Newport Oriel mc-Si reference solar cell.(E) EQE spectrum and device characteristics of the mc-Si solar cell. (A, B) was adapted from RГ¶hr etВ al.1; (C)В was adapted from RГ¶hr etВ al.2

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: This protocol includes Python code used for calculating the power settings to match simulator LEDs to the underwater spectra, translation to reference conditions, and spectral mismatch calculations. Additional code for calculating the water-attenuated irradiance spectra has also been given if existing data is not available.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: While all the used data can be found on the GitHub repository, below we provide steps as to how the data can be obtained directly from the primary sources. So, while not strictly necessary, we strongly encourage the reader to go through the steps outlined below.

steps.title: Math used for the current-correction procedure
steps.instruction: The simulated spectrum deviates slightly from the desired underwater spectrum, requiring a calibration step to accurately simulate the current characteristics of the test device. This calibration is easily accomplished by adjusting the distance between the device and the simulator using an adjustable lab jack and the calibrated reference device.

steps.title: Math used for the current-correction procedure
steps.instruction: The provided code, SpectraMaximus, takes the LED outputs, underwater spectra, reference spectrum (AM1.5G), reference device EQE, and test device EQE to calculate a target short circuit current for the reference cell, at which height the following relation is true:(EquationВ 3)IDUTLEDIDUTUW=1

steps.title: Math used for the current-correction procedure
steps.instruction: By defining a spectrum calibration number, CN, for the underwater and reference spectra as in Osterwald etВ al.6 we define the current of the reference cell under an arbitrary underwater spectrum. Dividing these two equations together obtains EquationВ 5.(EquationВ 4)CNUW=IREFUWEUW,CNRC=IREFRCEtotx(EquationВ 5)IREFUW=IREFRCCNUWГ—EUWCNRCГ—Etot

steps.title: Math used for the current-correction procedure
steps.instruction: Substituting integrated spectral response and integrated spectral irradiance into the equations for calibration number, we obtain:(EquationВ 6)CNRC=вҲ«О»1О»2SRREF(О»)Г—ОөREF(О»)dО»вҲ«О»1О»1ОөRC(О»)dО»(EquationВ 7)CNUW=вҲ«О»1О»2SRREF(О»)Г—ОөUW(О»)dО»вҲ«О»1О»1ОөUW(О»)dО»

steps.title: Math used for the current-correction procedure
steps.instruction: By substituting these equations into EquationВ 5 and canceling the appropriate terms, we obtain a simple ratio of two integrals multiplied by the reference current to obtain IREFUW.(EquationВ 8)IREFUW=IREFRCГ—вҲ«О»1О»2SRREF(О»)Г—ОөUW(О»)dО»вҲ«О»1О»2SRREF(О»)Г—ОөRC(О»)dО»

steps.title: Math used for the current-correction procedure
steps.instruction: Having obtained the relevant underwater current for the reference device, we must now calculate the spectral mismatch7 coefficient M between the underwater spectrum and the simulated LED spectrum. M is defined as the ratio of four currents in Osterwald etВ al.6 We solve for the desired current IREFLED and apply the current matching condition in EquationВ 12.(EquationВ 9)M=IDUTLEDIREFLEDГ—IREFUWIDUTUW(EquationВ 10)M=вҲ«О»1О»2ОөLED(О»)Г—SRDUT(О»)dО»вҲ«О»1О»2ОөLED(О»)Г—SRREF(О»)dО»Г—вҲ«О»1О»2ОөUW(О»)Г—SRREF(О»)dО»вҲ«О»1О»2ОөUW(О»)Г—SRDUT(О»)dО».

steps.title: Math used for the current-correction procedure
steps.instruction: SR stands for spectral response and it is related to the cellвҖҷs EQE, which is one of the initial inputs into SpectraMaximus. The conversion of the reference and test cell EQEs to SRREF and SRDUT (FigureВ 7) is found by the following equation, where q is the elementary charge, О» is the wavelength, h is PlanckвҖҷs constant, and c is the speed of light:(EquationВ 11)SR(О»)=qО»hcEQE(О»)=О»1239.8EQE(О»)for i in range (0,len(Test_EQEs)):В test= pd.read_csv(Test_EQEs[i],header=0,names=['wv','qe'])В if iВ == 0: df_EQE['wv']В = test['wv']В test['sr']В = ((test['qe'])вҲ—(test['wv'] / 1239.8))FigureВ 7Spectral response and EQESRDUT of the CdTe cell (blue) and the corresponding EQE (dotted) compared to SRRC of the mc-Si reference cell.

steps.title: Math used for the current-correction procedure
steps.instruction: When EquationВ 9 is manipulated to isolate for IREFLED, the obtained value is the short-circuit current that the reference device will register when it is at the height for which the current matching condition (EquationВ 3) is true, ensuring an accurate current is measured in the test device.(EquationВ 12)IREFLED=IREFUWM

steps.instruction: The code describing the above math is shown below.for Wavelength_name in name_list:В Simulation_spectrumВ = np.dot(L,df_LEDset[wv_name])В Wavelength_rangeВ = np.linspace(start_nm, end_nm,вҲ–В num=(end_nm-start_nm+1))В #---------------Interpolation ----- ----------------В # . . . Refer to code . . . |В # --------------------------------------------------В #----------Spectral Mismatch Calculation -----------В . . .В int1В = np.trapz(SR_ref вҲ— E_uws)В int2В = np.trapz(SR_ref вҲ— E_sim)В int3В = np.trapz(SR_test вҲ— E_sim)В int4В = np.trapz(SR_test вҲ— E_uws)В int5В = np.trapz(SR_ref вҲ— E_am15g)В hВ = int3/int4В E_simВ = E_sim/h .В .В .#-------------Spectral Mismatch Calculation --------- mВ = (int1/int2) вҲ— (int3/int4) .В .В .#--------Short Circuit Current Correction------------ I_uw_refВ = I_rc_refвҲ—(int1/int5) I_sim_refВ = I_uw_ref/m
